reasoning: Peer set: dark, cool-toned metallic pixel-art helmet/armor icons with thin outlines, multi-layer metallic shading, subtle highlights, high detail density, and a neutral-to-dark palette (grays, blacks, deep reds, muted teals, dark golds). Candidate: bright warm-orange/amber box with a face, flat retro-style rendering, white-flash highlights on top face, minimal shading layers, very low detail density, and a saturated warm palette (orange, yellow, blue accent) that shares almost no hue overlap with peers. (1) Outline weight — candidate outlines are thicker and blockier vs peers' crisp 1px metallic strokes. (2) Outline color — both dark, marginal pass. (3) Shadow direction — peers lit upper-left casting shadow lower-right; candidate has a bright top-face implying top-down lighting, inconsistent. (4) Shadow count — peers use 2-3 gradient shadow steps; candidate has at most 1 step, feels flat. (5) Highlight intensity — candidate's top-face white highlight is far too intense vs the subtle specular glints on peers. (6) Color temperature — critical failure: peers are cool/neutral metallic, candidate is hot orange/amber; largest single gap. (7) Detail density — peers pack rivets, plume geometry, visor slots into the same icon footprint; candidate is large flat color blocks with one face graphic. (8) Proportion — both icons at comparable pixel scale, acceptable. (9) Rendering style — peers use semi-realistic metallic pixel art; candidate is flat retro-cartoon, entirely different vocabulary. (10) Palette overlap — near-zero shared hues; the only shared element is the dark outline. (11) Charm level — peers are battle-gear serious; candidate is cute/chibi face-box. If Echoes are supposed to be cute vs equipment peers that is a category design choice, but the rendering and palette fundamentals still mismatch the family severely. Overall the candidate reads as a sprite from a completely different game's asset library dropped into this icon sheet.
